title: privacy.tsx duplicate import + dead variable
body_excerpt: `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/more/privacy.tsx` â€” `useLinkedChildren` is imported on both line 12 and line 19. Duplicate import will fail ESLint `no-duplicate-imports`. Additionally, `showFamilyConsentControls` is assigned
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/more/privacy.tsx: `useLinkedChildren` appears only once (line 12). No `showFamilyConsentControls` variable exists in the file. Both issues are gone.

title: handleMoreTabPress double-navigation (_layout.tsx)
body_excerpt: `handleMoreTabPress` double-navigation (`_layout.tsx`). Every other tab listener only calls `queryClient.invalidateQueries`. Only the More tab additionally calls `router.replace` â€” Expo Router already navigates
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_layout.tsx (1852 lines): no `handleMoreTabPress`, no `router.replace('/(app)/more')` in a tab listener. Grep for `tabPress`, `router.replace.*more`, and `handleMoreTabPress` all return no matches. Double-navigation code is gone.

title: accommodation.tsx redirect fires before profile loads
body_excerpt: `accommodation.tsx` redirect fires before profile loads. `canEditChildPreferences` is `false` when `activeProfile` is still `null` (cold load). With `isChildMode = true` from URL params, the `useEffect` bounces the owner
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/more/accommodation.tsx lines 64-69: `useEffect` now starts with `if (!activeProfile) return;` guard before the redirect check. This matches the suggested fix exactly.

title: Dead-end on final question while auto-save is in-flight (play.tsx)
body_excerpt: Dead-end on final question while auto-save is in-flight (`play.tsx`). When the last question is answered, `submitRound({ navigateOnSuccess: false })` fires and sets `roundSubmittedRef.current = true`. If the user taps Continue
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx lines 604-614: `handleContinue` now checks `if (roundAutoSaved)` (navigate immediately), then `if (roundAutoSaveStarted)` (sets `pendingResultsNavigateRef.current = true` and returns). The `onSuccess` handler at line 222 checks `navigateOnSuccess || pendingResultsNavigateRef.current` to trigger navigation. Dead-end eliminated.

title: play.tsx â€” handleSaveAndQuit references submitRound before its useCallback declaration
body_excerpt: `play.tsx` â€” `handleSaveAndQuit` is a plain `const` at ~line 239 but `submitRound` (a `useCallback`) is not declared until ~line 259. JavaScript `const` is not hoisted like `function`, so `no-use-before-define` will flag this.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx: `submitRound` useCallback is declared at line 201; `handleSaveAndQuit` is at line 262. `submitRound` is declared well before `handleSaveAndQuit`. Ordering violation fixed.

title: _clearProviders() in afterAll without worker isolation
body_excerpt: `_clearProviders()` in `afterAll` without worker isolation. Three files now call `_clearProviders()` in `afterAll`. If Jest shares a worker between suites, teardown of one suite can clear the registry while another suite
evidence: apps/api/src/services/book-suggestion-generation.integration.test.ts: uses `registerLlmProviderFixture()` which returns a `dispose()` that calls `unregisterProvider(fixture.provider.id)` (scoped teardown). The `afterAll` at line 119-123 calls `llmFixture.dispose()` â€” no global `_clearProviders()` call. The `llm-provider-fixtures.ts` `dispose()` method uses `unregisterProvider` rather than clearing all providers.

title: bookId param round-trip unverified
body_excerpt: `bookId` param round-trip unverified. `book/[bookId].tsx` now passes `bookId` to `topic/[topicId]` on all three push paths. Confirm `[topicId].tsx` actually reads `bookId` and passes it through to session-summary navigation
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/topic/[topicId].tsx lines 268-273: `bookId: paramBookId` is destructured from `useLocalSearchParams`. Line 289: used in fallback href (`/(app)/shelf/[subjectId]/book/[bookId]`). Line 508: forwarded to session navigation via `...(paramBookId ? { bookId: paramBookId } : {})`. Round-trip is wired end-to-end.

title: Accommodation screen redirects legitimate owners during profile load
body_excerpt: Accommodation screen redirects legitimate owners during profile load. File: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/more/accommodation.tsx, lines 80-84. The useEffect fires as soon as `isChildMode && !canEditChildPreferences`. During the initial render, activeProfile is null
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/more/accommodation.tsx lines 64-69: `useEffect` guard `if (!activeProfile) return;` is present at the top of the effect, before the redirect check. Dependency array includes `activeProfile`. This is the same finding as comment-4433688582 Critical #2, and the fix is verified in code.

title: canEditChildPreferences depends on activeProfile â€” premature redirect on cold load
body_excerpt: `canEditChildPreferences` depends on `activeProfile` and `childProfile`, both from `useProfile()`. On first render, `profiles` may be empty and `activeProfile` may be null/undefined while the hook resolves. At that moment `canEditChildPreferences` evaluates to `false`
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/more/accommodation.tsx line 65: `if (!activeProfile) return;` guards the redirect. This is a duplicate of the same accommodation.tsx redirect finding caught by two different reviewers; the fix covers both.

title: play.tsx â€” handleSeeResults/handleOneMore silent guard + integration-mock-guard filesystem walk + invalidateSessionDerivedQueries scope
body_excerpt: `play.tsx` â€” Both handlers do `if (!roundAutoSaved) return;` and silently do nothing if called while the auto-save is still in flight. The JSX at line ~984 correctly gates the button section on `roundAutoSaved`
evidence: The handleSeeResults/handleOneMore tap-window issue is addressed (buttons hidden during roundAutoSaveStarted). The invalidateSessionDerivedQueries scope is fixed in use-sessions.ts (useSetSessionInputMode now only invalidates session-transcript). However, integration-mock-guard.test.ts still uses readdirSync filesystem walk (line 43) instead of `git ls-files`, with no comment explaining the CI-container rationale. This sub-issue remains unaddressed as flagged.
suggested_notion_title: integration-mock-guard.test.ts uses filesystem walk instead of git ls-files â€” no rationale documented
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/test-utils/integration-mock-guard.test.ts uses `readdirSync` (line 43) to enumerate integration test files instead of the original `git ls-files`. The CCR noted this means untracked and `.gitignore`d files can be scanned, causing local scratch files to trigger the guard even if they never affect CI. The original `git ls-files` was more precise for CI-enforcement intent. There is no comment in the file explaining why the switch was made (e.g., 'CI containers may not have git'). Fix: either revert to `git ls-files` or add a comment documenting why `readdirSync` is preferred, e.g. `// Using readdirSync because CI PostgreSQL containers may not have git available`.

title: Quit modal 'keep playing' button uses wrong i18n key (quiz.play.oneMore vs quiz.play.keepPlaying)
body_excerpt: Quit modal 'keep playing' button uses the wrong i18n key. File: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx, lines 1147-1156. When hasAnsweredQuestions is true, the primary quit-dialog button renders t('quiz.play.oneMore')
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx lines 1138-1140: when `hasAnsweredQuestions` is true, the cancel/keep-playing button still renders `t('quiz.play.oneMore')` â€” 'Wait, just one more!' â€” instead of `t('quiz.play.keepPlaying')`. The `quiz.play.keepPlaying` key is used in the zero-answers branch (line 1140 else branch) but the hasAnsweredQuestions branch was not updated.
suggested_notion_title: Quit modal 'keep playing' button uses wrong i18n key when hasAnsweredQuestions=true
suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx lines 1132-1141: when `hasAnsweredQuestions` is true, the primary quit-dialog cancel button renders `t('quiz.play.oneMore')` ('Wait, just one more!') which semantically means 'start another quiz round'. The correct key for 'stay in the current round' is `t('quiz.play.keepPlaying')`, which is already used in the zero-answers branch. Fix: change both the text (line 1139) and accessibilityLabel (line 1132) in the `hasAnsweredQuestions` branch from `t('quiz.play.oneMore')` to `t('quiz.play.keepPlaying')`. Both locale files already have the key â€” no i18n additions needed.

title: handleSaveAndQuit is a silent no-op when auto-save is already in flight
body_excerpt: handleSaveAndQuit is a silent no-op when auto-save is already in flight. File: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx, lines 241-244. When the last question is answered, submitRound fires automatically and sets roundSubmittedRef.current = true
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx lines 262-266: `handleSaveAndQuit` calls `submitRound()` directly without checking `roundSubmittedRef.current`. When auto-save is in flight, `submitRound` hits `if (roundSubmittedRef.current) return` at line 206 â€” the modal closes (`setQuitConfirmVisible(false)`) but no navigation occurs, and the onSuccess auto-save handler fires with `navigateOnSuccess:false` and `pendingResultsNavigateRef.current=false`, so results never navigate. User is stuck post-modal-close. Suggested fix: add `if (roundSubmittedRef.current) { return; }` before `submitRound()` in `handleSaveAndQuit`, relying on the already-started auto-save's `onSuccess` to navigate.
suggested_notion_title: Quit dialog 'Save and Finish' leaves user stuck when auto-save is in flight (play.tsx)

title: gc1-allow annotation is inside the factory body, not on the jest.mock() line
body_excerpt: gc1-allow annotation is inside the factory body, not on the jest.mock() line. File: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/child/[profileId]/_layout.test.tsx, lines 30-32. CLAUDE.md rule: append // gc1-allow: reason on the same line as the jest.mock( call
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/child/[profileId]/_layout.test.tsx lines 30-32: `jest.mock('../../../../lib/theme', () => ({` on line 30, then `// gc1-allow: theme hook requires native ColorScheme unavailable in JSDOM` as a standalone comment on line 31 inside the factory. CLAUDE.md requires the annotation on the same line as `jest.mock(`. The GC1 ratchet scans for `// gc1-allow:` on the `jest.mock(` line itself, not inside the factory body.
suggested_notion_title: gc1-allow annotation in child/_layout.test.tsx is inside factory body, not on jest.mock() line
suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/child/[profileId]/_layout.test.tsx lines 30-32: the `// gc1-allow:` annotation is a standalone comment inside the factory body, not on the `jest.mock(` line. CLAUDE.md requires: `jest.mock('../../../../lib/theme' /* gc1-allow: theme hook requires native ColorScheme unavailable in JSDOM */, () => ({`. The GC1 ratchet checks for the annotation on the `jest.mock(` call line. As written, if the GC1 lint rule is tightened to require inline annotation, this site will become a new violation.

title: Expo Router tab press double-navigation / cross-stack deep push without ancestor chain
body_excerpt: Expo Router's tab press already pops to the stack root for the current tab and switches tabs for inactive ones. Calling `router.replace('/(app)/more')` inside the listener fires a second navigation action
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_layout.tsx: no `router.replace('/(app)/more')` in any tab listener. The tab press double-navigation is gone. The architectural note about `LearnerScreen.tsx` cross-stack push to `/(app)/progress/[subjectId]` without ancestor chain is a documentation gap but the key correctness concern (double-navigation) is eliminated.

title: Toggling voice/text mode causes unnecessary invalidations via invalidateSessionDerivedQueries
body_excerpt: Toggling voice/text mode during an active session does not affect `progress`, `dashboard`, `retention`, `language-progress`, or `resume-nudge`. Adding `invalidateSessionDerivedQueries` here causes 5 unnecessary background refetches
evidence: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts lines 217-228: `useSetSessionInputMode.onSuccess` now only calls `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['session-transcript', sessionId] })` with a comment explaining why `invalidateSessionDerivedQueries` was removed ('Input mode changes only mutate the session row/transcript metadata'). The unnecessary 5-query invalidation is gone.

title: Hard-timeout timer does not reset when user retries while mutation is still pending â€” launch.tsx
body_excerpt: Hard-timeout timer does not reset when the user retries while mutation is still pending â€” launch.tsx. Both useEffect timeout hooks depend only on [generateRound.isPending]. When the 30s hard-timeout fires and the user presses Retry
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/launch.tsx lines 236-246: both timeout effects (`timedOut` at line 224-231, `hardTimedOut` at line 233-246) depend only on `[generateRound.isPending]`. The Retry handler at line 309-310 calls `setHardTimedOut(false); startRound()` but TanStack Query does not change `isPending` (stays `true`), so neither effect re-runs. No `retryCount` state variable exists. On slow network, retrying after a timeout arms no new timer.
suggested_notion_title: Quiz launch: hard-timeout and soft-nudge timers do not re-arm after Retry on slow network
suggested_notion_body:
apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/launch.tsx: the `timedOut` (20s) and `hardTimedOut` (30s) useEffect hooks depend only on `[generateRound.isPending]`. When a timeout fires and the user presses Retry, `startRound()` re-fires the mutation but `isPending` remains `true` throughout â€” neither effect re-runs and no new timer is armed. A user on a slow network who hits Retry after the hard-timeout will never see the hard-timeout panel again; the only escape is Cancel.

Fix: add `const [retryCount, setRetryCount] = useState(0)` and call `setRetryCount(c => c + 1)` in the Retry handler. Add `retryCount` to both timeout-effect dep arrays so effects re-arm on each deliberate retry.

title: Hardcoded English accessibility labels on final-question action buttons â€” play.tsx
body_excerpt: Hardcoded English accessibility labels on final-question action buttons â€” play.tsx. Five accessibilityLabel props added in this PR use literal English strings instead of t(): 'See quiz results', 'Start one more quiz', 'Next question'
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx: hardcoded English accessibilityLabel values are still present: line 967 'See quiz results', line 981 'Start one more quiz', line 1001 'Next question', line 1031 'Dispute this answer', line 1148 'Save progress and finish round'. None route through `t()`. The project ships 7 locale files; screen-reader users on non-English locales will always hear these in English.
suggested_notion_title: Hardcoded English accessibilityLabels on quiz play screen final-question buttons
suggested_notion_body:
apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/play.tsx has 5 hardcoded English `accessibilityLabel` strings instead of `t()` calls:
- Line 967: `"See quiz results"` â†’ `t('quiz.play.seeResultsLabel')` (or reuse `t('quiz.play.seeResults')`)
- Line 981: `"Start one more quiz"` â†’ `t('quiz.play.oneMoreLabel')` (or `t('quiz.play.oneMore')`)
- Line 1001: `"Next question"` â†’ `t('quiz.play.nextQuestionLabel')` (or `t('quiz.play.nextQuestion')`)
- Line 1031: `"Dispute this answer"` â†’ `t('quiz.play.disputeLabel')`
- Line 1148: `"Save progress and finish round"` â†’ `t('quiz.play.saveAndFinishLabel')` (or `t('quiz.play.saveAndFinish')`)

Add new i18n keys to all 7 locale files (`en`, `de`, `es`, `nb`, `pt`, `pl`, `ja`) under `quiz.play.*`.

title: docs/audit/handover.md:17 commits author's absolute local filesystem path
body_excerpt: `docs/audit/handover.md:17` commits the author's absolute local filesystem path: `/Users/vetinari/_dev/eduagent-build` and `~/.archon/workspaces/cognoco/eduagent-build/worktrees/archon/thread-*`
evidence: docs/_archive/consistency-cleanup/audit/handover.md (file moved from docs/audit/handover.md): no `/Users/vetinari/_dev/eduagent-build` absolute path present. The file now references `.archon/workspaces/cognoco/eduagent-build/worktrees/archon/thread-*/` (relative/workspace-relative path) and has a Status: CLOSED banner at line 3. The machine-specific absolute path has been removed.

title: handover.md describes past work with no obsolescence signal
body_excerpt: The document is addressed to 'an agent picking up the remaining C9 cluster work.' That work is closed in this same PR. Once merged the document describes past work, and nothing in it signals its own obsolescence.
evidence: docs/_archive/consistency-cleanup/audit/handover.md line 3: `> **Status: CLOSED 2026-05-13.** All three PRs addressed in [PR #239 ...]`. The document has a prominent Status: CLOSED banner at the top. The file has also been moved to the `_archive/` directory.

title: Two API source files still point to plan paths that no longer exist after PR-29 moves
body_excerpt: Two API source files still point to plan paths that no longer exist after the PR-29 moves: `apps/api/src/test-utils/route-metering-fixture.ts:4` â†’ `docs/plans/c1-mock-inventory` and `apps/api/src/route-utils/route-context.ts:9` â†’ `docs/plans/2026-05-03-governance-audit.md`
evidence: apps/api/src/test-utils/route-metering-fixture.ts line 4: comment references `docs/_archive/plans/done/2026-05-04-c1-mock-inventory/` â€” the directory exists. apps/api/src/route-utils/route-context.ts line 9: comment references `docs/_archive/plans/done/2026-05-03-governance-audit.md` â€” the file exists. Both paths are now the current `_archive` locations. The stale references were updated.

title: Mermaid loaded from jsdelivr with floating @11 tag and no SRI hash
body_excerpt: The file loads Mermaid from `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@11/...` using a floating major-version tag (`@11`), without a Subresource Integrity hash. Anyone who opens the file in a browser executes whatever jsdelivr serves at that tag
evidence: docs/_archive/consistency-cleanup/audit/pr-dispatch-graph.html line 359: `import mermaid from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@11/dist/mermaid.esm.min.mjs"` â€” still uses floating `@11` tag with no `integrity` attribute. No SRI hash added, no version pinning, no offline SVG alternative.
suggested_notion_title: pr-dispatch-graph.html loads Mermaid from jsdelivr with floating version tag and no SRI hash
suggested_notion_body:
docs/_archive/consistency-cleanup/audit/pr-dispatch-graph.html line 359: loads Mermaid from `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@11/dist/mermaid.esm.min.mjs` with a floating `@11` major-version tag and no Subresource Integrity hash. Anyone opening this file in a browser executes whatever jsdelivr serves at that tag â€” a supply-chain risk if the CDN is compromised.

Fix options (in order of preference): (1) Pin to an exact version with an `integrity` attribute (e.g. `integrity="sha384-..." crossorigin="anonymous"`); (2) Render the Mermaid diagram offline and commit the SVG, removing the CDN dependency entirely; (3) Pin the exact semver in the URL (e.g. `mermaid@11.4.1`). Since the file is now in `_archive`, option 2 (static SVG) is simplest.
